name: Clam Chowder - from a New England Famous Rest
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the salt pork into small dice and render it in a saucepan until rendered.
2. Reserve the cracklings (crispy bits) from the rendered fat.
3. In the same pan, cook the onion thinly sliced until golden brown.
4. Bring water to a boil in another pot.
5. Add peeled and cubed potatoes to the boiling water and cook for 10 minutes.
6. Remove the potatoes from the water and add chopped clams. Cook for an additional 25 minutes.
7. Stir in milk, cracklings (reserving some for garnish), cooked potatoes, onion, butter, salt, and pepper to taste.
8. Serve the clam chowder in soup bowls topped with cracker crumbs.
